_symbols_note: List of underlyings to trade concurrently, e.g. ["SPX", "XSP"]. Each symbol gets its own live ATM subscription window and its own GEX channel (net_gex/gamma_flip/call_wall/put_wall computed from that symbol's own OI+greeks) — there is no longer a separate gex_symbol; GEX is 1:1 with every traded symbol. The single-symbol 'symbol' key is still accepted as a deprecated alias for ["symbol"] if 'symbols' is absent, but new configs should use 'symbols'. max_concurrent_ics and max_entries_per_day are account-wide totals shared across every symbol in this list, not per-symbol caps. Correlated symbols (e.g. SPX and XSP) are not currently exposure-limited against each other — avoid combinations that double up on the same directional risk until that guard exists.
_delta_target_note: Superseded by the VIX-banded scale below when the market-wide VIX (fetched once per iteration in Step 4a) falls in a defined band -- delta_target itself is the fallback only if VIX is unavailable. See delta_target_vix_low/elevated/high/crisis and vix_band_low_max/elevated_max/high_max.
_delta_target_vix_bands_note: VIX <= vix_band_low_max (18): delta_target_vix_low (0.16). VIX <= vix_band_elevated_max (25): delta_target_vix_elevated (0.14). VIX <= vix_band_high_max (35): delta_target_vix_high (0.12). VIX > vix_band_high_max: delta_target_vix_crisis (0.10) -- note VIX > regime_vix_pause_threshold (25) already pauses new IC entries account-wide, so the high/crisis bands mostly affect what a scan *would* have targeted, not what actually gets entered; kept for consistency and for ORB debit-spread strike selection, which isn't blocked by the VIX regime gate. Documented trader convention (VIX-regime delta scaling), not a backtested-optimal breakpoint set for this specific strategy -- treat as a reasoned starting point, not a proven-optimal one.
_max_wing_width_note: Upper bound (points) on spread width; the agent chooses the actual wing width per entry (any reasonable value up to this max) based on credit, buying power, and session conditions rather than scanning a fixed list.
_cash_settled_symbols_note: Symbols that settle in cash at expiration. All positions are force-closed at force_close_time regardless of membership here — this list only controls whether a missed force-close is escalated as a critical assignment-risk failure (symbol not listed) or routine cash-settlement remediation (symbol listed). Leave any equity or physically-settled symbol you add to 'symbols' out of this list.
_enable_live_trading_note: Set to true to allow execute_trade/adjust_order/close_position to submit real orders. Requires tastytrade OAuth credentials in the OS keyring (via `python src/tt.py secrets_set`). Left false in this example so a fresh setup starts in dry-run.
_daily_ic_trade_target_note: Target number of IC entries per day. This is a guidance heuristic, not a hard cap — buying power, max_concurrent_ics, and regime gates remain the binding constraints. The agent uses this target to calibrate position sizing and entry selectivity (e.g., if target is 2 and 1 is already open, remaining slots are reserved for only high-conviction setups). Set to 0 to disable IC entries and run ORB only.
_risk_profiles_note: Rather than hand-editing config keys, use /set-risk-profile to switch between named presets: conservative (default, safest), moderate (recommended after 2–4 weeks), aggressive (after 2+ weeks at moderate with 60%+ win rate), very-aggressive (deliberate short experiments only). Each profile bundles related gate thresholds with offsetting position-cap and stop-management constraints. See config.risk.json and docs/risk-profiles.md for full rationale.
_entry_price_strategy_note: auto (default): agent chooses per-iteration based on session, spread width, and IV rank rather than always trying mid first and eating the wait. mid: Day limit at streaming mid price; falls back to natural_bid if avg per-leg spread > mid_spread_gate or session is open_volatile/late, or if unfilled after mid_improve_wait_seconds. natural_bid: immediate fill price. ioc_step: IOC attempts above natural bid stepping down. day_improve: Day limit at natural_bid + day_improve_amount.
_separate_spread_entry_note: Controls IC order structure. false = always 4-leg combo. true = always separate 2-leg spreads. "auto" = agent evaluates per-iteration: favors separate when IV rank > 0.35, session is late/open_volatile, or 2+ ICs already open; falls back to separate if combo dry_run returns warnings.
_min_credit_pct_of_width_note: Minimum credit as fraction of wing width; reject entries below this (e.g. 2-wide must collect >= $0.40).
_low_iv_note: When IV rank is >= min_iv_rank but <= low_iv_credit_floor_iv_rank_max, the credit floor relaxes to low_iv_min_credit_pct_of_width instead of min_credit_pct_of_width.
_min_net_credit_after_fees_note: The fee-adjusted floor no longer uses a flat dollar constant (retired 2026-07-06). It now requires (ic_natural_bid * dollar_multiplier) - est_fee_per_contract to clear the same applicable pct-of-width floor above, converted to dollars. The old standalone min_credit absolute-dollar-per-symbol floor was also retired as redundant once this fee check became width/IV-aware.
_fee_estimate_fallback_per_contract_note: Bootstrap $/contract-set fee estimate per symbol, used until fee_estimate_min_sample_size closed trades exist for that symbol; then the DB-derived average from get_fee_estimate takes over.
_stop_type_note: spread (default, and currently the only supported mode): software stop monitors the IC's cost and closes the spread when the trigger ratio is hit. tastytrade does not support exchange-level multi-leg stop orders for combos.
_stop_trigger_ratio_note: Fraction of net_credit at which a per-side stop triggers. Default 0.95 = stop fires when that side's cost reaches 95% of the total IC credit.
_stop_limit_ratio_note: Cushion multiplier on the marketable closing debit ((short_ask - long_bid) * stop_limit_ratio) so the Day limit stays marketable even if the quote ticks against you before the order reaches the exchange.
_profit_target_pct_note: Close an IC when its current cost drops to <= this fraction of net_credit (0.50 = 50% profit captured).
_otm_pct_note: OTM distance as a fraction of underlying price, e.g. (strike - underlying_price) / underlying_price for calls. Percentage-based so no rescaling is needed if symbol changes.
_regime_vix1d_ratio_note: vix1d / vix ratio, computed once per iteration in Step 4a (market-wide, like VIX -- not symbol-specific). Documented trader convention: ratio >= 1.0 is an 'expansion day' (above-average move priced in), >= 1.30 is an 'event day' (Fed/CPI/shock priced in -- not the environment for short premium without substantial width and reduced size), <= 0.75 is a 'compression day'. 1.30 is used here as the IC-entry pause threshold, same role as regime_vix_pause_threshold but a same-day-specific signal rather than a 30-day-window one. Not independently backtested for this strategy -- a reasoned starting point from documented convention, not a proven-optimal breakpoint.
